qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended general-purpose user interface for a JupyterHub?
qa_pairs.answer: JupyterLab is the recommended general-purpose user interface to use on a JupyterHub.

qa_pairs.question: What functionalities does a JupyterLab server provide?
qa_pairs.answer: From a JupyterLab server, you can manage your remote files and folders and launch Jupyter applications like a terminal, Python 3 notebooks, RStudio, and a Linux desktop.

qa_pairs.question: Can users add their own kernels in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users can add their own 'kernels', which appear as application tiles. Configuration details can be found in the 'Adding kernels' section of the JupyterNotebook documentation.

qa_pairs.question: What are the primary ways to launch JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: JupyterLab can be launched traditionally using JupyterHub, or more recently, through Open OnDemand, which some sites have deployed.

qa_pairs.question: Which clusters provide a fully-featured JupyterHub for JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: The Fir, Narval, and Rorqual clusters provide a fully-featured JupyterHub for JupyterLab.

qa_pairs.question: On which clusters can JupyterLab be launched via Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: JupyterLab can be launched via Open OnDemand on the Nibi, Trillium, and Vulcan clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any specific requirements for launching JupyterLab on the Trillium cluster via Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, on the Trillium cluster, you need to request 'Jupyter Lab + Alliance software extensions' when launching JupyterLab via Open OnDemand.

qa_pairs.question: Which clusters do not currently offer JupyterLab through either JupyterHub or Open OnDemand?
qa_pairs.answer: The Killarney and tamIA clusters do not currently offer JupyterLab access via JupyterHub or Open OnDemand.

qa_pairs.question: Is it recommended to install JupyterLab in a virtual environment manually?
qa_pairs.answer: No, installing JupyterLab yourself in a virtual environment is not recommended, as you will not benefit from any of the pre-configured applications.

qa_pairs.question: What does 'Fully-featured' indicate regarding JupyterLab availability on clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Fully-featured' column indicates whether the JupyterLab interface available has all of the features described in the document.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of dashboard is presented when opening JupyterLab in recent clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: When you open JupyterLab in one of our most recent clusters, you will be presented with a dashboard pre-populated with a few launchers.

qa_pairs.question: What are some of the default launchers found in the JupyterLab dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: Default launchers include Python 3.11, LibreQDA, Mate Desktop (VNC), OpenRefine, RStudio, VS Code, and XFCE4 Desktop (VNC).

qa_pairs.question: What additional links might be present on the JupyterLab dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: In addition to launchers, you may find links to the cluster's Globus collection, its job portal, and relevant documentation pages.

qa_pairs.question: How do loaded modules affect the JupyterLab dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: By loading modules, new launchers will appear in the dashboard.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user close their JupyterLab session?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can close their JupyterLab session through the 'File' menu by selecting either 'Hub Control Panel' to manually stop the server and job, or 'Log Out' to end the session, server, and corresponding job.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the 'Hub Control Panel' option in the File menu?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Hub Control Panel' option allows users to manually stop the JupyterLab server and its corresponding job on the cluster, which is useful for starting a new server with different resources.

qa_pairs.question: What happens when you select 'Log Out' from the JupyterLab File menu?
qa_pairs.answer: Selecting 'Log Out' will end the session, which will also stop the JupyterLab server and the corresponding job on the cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What is the function of the tool selector on the left side of the JupyterLab interface?
qa_pairs.answer: The tool selector on the left side of the JupyterLab interface changes the content of the frame on the right.

qa_pairs.question: What can users do with the 'File Browser' (folder icon) tool?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'File Browser' tool allows users to browse their home, project, and scratch spaces, and it can also be used to upload files.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the 'Running Terminals and Kernels' (stop icon) tool?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Running Terminals and Kernels' tool is used to stop kernel sessions and terminal sessions.

qa_pairs.question: When would a user access the 'GPU Dashboards' (GPU card icon) tool?
qa_pairs.answer: If your job uses GPUs, the 'GPU Dashboards' tool will provide access to some resource monitoring options.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary function of the 'Software Modules' tool?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Software Modules' tool is where you can load or unload software modules available in our environment. Depending on the modules loaded, icons for corresponding Jupyter applications will appear in the 'Launcher' tab.

qa_pairs.question: Does Nibi offer a fully-featured JupyterLab interface?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Nibi provides access to a fully-featured JupyterLab interface through Open OnDemand.

qa_pairs.question: Is the JupyterLab interface available on Vulcan fully-featured?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Vulcan provides access to a fully-featured JupyterLab interface through Open OnDemand.
